import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut cur = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10
        if has
          if neg
            result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
          else
            result = list.push(result, cur)
          end
          cur = 0
          has = false
          neg = false
        end
      else if b == 45
        neg = true
      else
        cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
        has = true
      end
    when None
      has = has
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
    else
      result = list.push(result, cur)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len() - 1
  mut found: Option<Int> = None
  mut done = false
  while not done and lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    match xs.get(mid)
    when Some(v)
      if v == t
        found = Some(mid)
        done = true
      else if v < t
        lo = mid + 1
      else
        hi = mid - 1
      end
    when None
      done = true
    end
  end
  found
end

fn read_or_empty() -> String
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
end

let line1 = read_or_empty()
let line2 = read_or_empty()
let xs = parse_ints(line1)
let trimmed = string.trim(line2)
match string.parse_int(trimmed)
when Some(t)
  match bsearch(xs, t)
  when Some(i)
    print("index=#{i}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end
when None
  print("not_found")
end
